findings.issue: The service worker caches every GET under /v1/ with a shared NetworkFirst cache, but those routes include Bearer-authenticated identity and organization data. Cache Storage keys are URL-centric, so a cached 200 for /v1/profile or another private endpoint can be replayed when the network is unavailable after logout or user switch, exposing stale authenticated data and potentially making the app treat the wrong user as signed in offline.
findings.remediation: Do not runtime-cache authenticated /v1/ API responses; at minimum exclude /v1/profile and other private endpoints from Workbox caching or require explicit no-store/cacheWillUpdate logic that refuses responses for requests carrying Authorization.

findings.issue: The requested test pass is not actually green: npm run test:e2e:ci exits non-zero because the Playwright global setup SC4 guard runs the main frontend Vitest suite and currently hits 15 failing assertions, including TodosBadge expecting an empty render while the component now renders a Queue button. This means the branch cannot satisfy its configured E2E/SC4 gate even though the remote-frontend unit suite passes.
findings.remediation: Fix the failing frontend assertions or the underlying regressions, then rerun npm run test:e2e:ci so the configured Playwright gate is green.
